mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows a negative or null coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive relationship reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading below 0.2% confirms disinflationary momentum, reducing expectations for aggressive central bank tightening and prompting immediate positive S&P 500 repricing within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings below 0.2% are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market fragility and hidden slack, signaling impending earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes positive during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands corporate revenue bases and improves forward earnings estimates, generating sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating supply-side momentum and easing capacity bottlenecks, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases > 0.5% are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold signal loses predictive power during recovery phases.
